title: Five Minutes with the Money
subtitle: A daily check-in activity for building a consistent money habit
excerpt: A simple daily activity: five minutes at the same time each day, looking at your money without judgement or task-management pressure. The aim is regularity, not productivity. The habit is the outcome.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 50 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to build a daily money habit but haven't found one that sticks.

content.text: Five minutes with the money, same time every day for one week. Open the banking app, look at the numbers, close it again. No spreadsheet required, no action items. The point is showing up, not sorting anything out.

Habits form through repetition at the same cue. Tacking this onto an existing morning routine (after the first coffee, before checking email) makes it easier to sustain than assigning it a fresh time slot.
Pick a fixed time. First thing in the morning works well because the mind is quiet.
Open the banking app or your tracking notebook. Look at the current balance. Notice what's there.
If a task occurs to you (move money, pay a bill), write it on a separate list. Don't do it now. Keep this five minutes as a check-in, not a task session.
Close the app. The five minutes is done.
The first few days may feel uneventful or slightly uncomfortable. The discomfort usually eases by day three or four, once the brain learns that looking is not the same as fixing.
Adapted from Day 50 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Pairs well with the Day 50 tapping practice and affirmation.
